Google Ads Management

Google Ads that protectyour margin, not Google's.

We audit, restructure, and manage Google accounts around one number: cost per qualified lead. Not clicks. Not impressions. The leads your sales team actually wants to talk to.

Audit Your Ad AccountView Our Work

Most Google Ads accounts bleed budget on the wrong searches

Google makes spending easy. Knowing what actually drives revenue is the hard part. Most accounts we audit have the same structural problems.

  • Broad match and auto-apply recommendations send budget to searches that will never convert.
  • Conversion tracking counts form fills and page views instead of leads your sales team confirms.
  • Brand, competitor, and generic terms sit in the same campaign, so you cannot tell what is working.
  • Landing pages do not match the ad promise, which kills Quality Score and raises your cost per click.

Google Ads built for Quality Score and ROAS

  • Separate campaigns for brand, competitor, and high-intent generic keywords.
  • Weekly search term reviews and negative keyword lists to cut waste before it compounds.
  • Ad copy and landing page alignment that improves Quality Score and lowers CPC.
  • Bid strategies tied to offline conversion data when your sales cycle runs longer than the click.

The Kinexus Campaign Framework

How we approach this work, and why it produces results most agencies don't.

01

Intent-first architecture

Brand, competitor, and high-intent searches each get their own campaign. No more one bucket for everything.

02

Weekly search term discipline

We pull the search terms report every week. Irrelevant queries get blocked before they eat another dollar.

03

Quality Score as a lever

When the ad and landing page say the same thing, Google charges you less per click and more people convert.

04

CRM-backed optimization

We bid toward leads your sales team actually closes, not form fills from people who were never going to buy.

Budget follows performance. Not habit.

We allocate spend based on what each channel is actually returning, not what felt right last quarter. High-ROAS channels get more fuel, underperformers get restructured or cut, and every reallocation is documented with a clear rationale.

Ads and landing pages that match.

When the ad promise carries through to the thank-you page, more people book and fewer leads drop off.

Audience targeting

First-party data and lookalikes reach people already searching for what you sell.

94% audience match

Message match

Landing pages echo the ad headline so Quality Score and conversion rate both improve.

8.2 avg Quality Score

Bid rules that protect margin

Automated rules cut wasted spend before it compounds.

32% less wasted spend

Scale with guardrails

Budget grows only when ROAS holds at your target.

4.5x avg ROAS

ROAS Dashboard

A live view of performance across every channel: spend, ROAS, and conversion rate, all updated in real time so decisions get made on current data, not last week's report.

The 5-phase ad system.

Every campaign follows a structured optimization cycle, from initial audit through scaling, so performance compounds over time rather than stalling after the launch week.

  1. Phase 1

    Audit & Structure

    Full account review. Campaigns organized by business goal, margin, and intent.

    Complete account audit in week 1

  2. Phase 2

    Audience & Keyword Build

    High-intent keywords + precise audience segments + ad copy that speaks directly to need.

    Avg. 240 high-intent keywords per campaign

  3. Phase 3

    Landing Page Alignment

    Ad-matched landing pages. Quality Score optimization. Conversion tracking verified.

    8.2 avg. Quality Score

  4. Phase 4

    Launch & Bid Setup

    Smart bidding, audience signals, extensions, negative keywords. Right setup from day one.

    Campaigns live within 14 days

  5. Phase 5

    Optimize & Scale

    Weekly bid adjustments. Search term mining. A/B testing. Quarterly performance reviews.

    ROAS tracked and reported weekly

Google Ads management deliverables

Search, Shopping, and Performance Max. Built for ROAS, not for Google's quarterly targets.

Scope of work

  • Account Audit and Restructure

    Full review of campaigns, ad groups, keywords, match types, and conversion actions with a rebuild plan.

  • Keyword Research and Negative Lists

    High-intent keyword targets plus ongoing negative keyword lists to block irrelevant traffic.

  • Ad Copy and Extension Management

    Responsive search ads, callouts, sitelinks, and structured snippets tested on a regular cadence.

  • Performance Max and Shopping Setup

    Feed optimization, asset groups, audience signals, and listing group structure for e-commerce accounts.

  • Conversion Tracking and Attribution

    GA4, Google Tag Manager, enhanced conversions, and CRM integration for accurate lead tracking.

  • Weekly Optimization and Reporting

    Bid adjustments, budget pacing, search term reviews, and monthly ROAS and CPL reporting.

Proof it works

Trust signals from real client work, not vanity metrics.

Case Study

Dental Practice

The last agency counted page views as conversions. Cost per lead kept climbing while the front desk stayed quiet.

New patient bookings up 340%. CPL down 45% in six months after we rebuilt tracking and campaign structure.

Transparent Pricing

Common Questions

FAQ

What budget do I need to get started?+

Most clients invest $2,500 to $15,000 per month in management plus ad spend. We tailor scope to your goals and provide clear pricing after a strategy call.

How quickly will I see results?+

Paid media produces data within days and meaningful optimization within 2 to 4 weeks. ROAS typically improves significantly by month 2 or 3 as we refine targeting and creative.

Do you require long-term contracts?+

No. We work month to month because results should earn retention, not contract lock-in.

How do you report performance?+

Weekly optimization notes and monthly dashboards covering spend, ROAS, CPL, conversion rate, and revenue attribution where tracking allows.

Which ad platforms do you manage?+

Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, and Microsoft Ads. We recommend the mix based on where your buyers actually convert, not platform hype.

Do you set up conversion tracking?+

Yes. GA4, GTM, call tracking, and offline conversion import are standard in week one. We do not optimize blind.

Can you work with our in-house team?+

Yes. We collaborate with internal marketers, other agencies, and fractional leaders. We can lead the program or fill a specific channel gap.

What industries do you serve?+

Home services, healthcare, professional services, SaaS, e-commerce, and manufacturing. If your buyers search before they buy, we can usually help.

How much does Google Ads management cost?+

Management fees start at $1,500 per month for accounts under $10,000 in ad spend. Larger accounts are priced at 12 to 15% of spend. Your ad budget is paid directly to Google. We recommend a minimum $3,000 monthly ad spend to gather enough data for optimization.

Do you manage Performance Max campaigns?+

Yes. We build and optimize Performance Max with proper asset groups, audience signals, and feed data for e-commerce. We also monitor search term insights and brand traffic to prevent PMax from cannibalizing your Search campaigns.

How quickly will I see results from Google Ads?+

Search campaigns often produce leads within the first week of launch. Stable, optimized CPL typically takes 30 to 45 days as we refine keywords, negatives, and bids. Shopping and PMax may need 2 to 3 weeks for the algorithm to stabilize.

Will I own my Google Ads account?+

Always. We work inside your account with full admin access. If you leave, you keep every campaign, keyword list, and conversion setup we built.

Let's Talk

Ready to Build a Revenue System?

No deck. No rehearsed presentation. Bring your actual situation: what's not working, what you've already tried, what you're hoping to fix. We'll have an honest conversation about whether we can help.

Book a Strategy Call

No long-term contracts. Month to month.
